_meta.purpose: Single source of truth for project context, decisions, architecture, and build progress. Read this file at the start of every session — before reading anything else — to fully restore context without re-deriving it from conversation history.
_meta.how_to_use: 1. Read this file. 2. Check build_checklist for current position. 3. Review open_questions. 4. Continue from the first incomplete checklist item after getting approval.
_meta.updated_by: Content improvement campaign complete (D011) — 5 PRs: standards/governance metadata, all 11 agents, 57 design skills, 34 engineering skills, 12 test skills + repo-wide verification. Next: Chunk 15.

project.name: SDLC Artifact Factory
project.author: Shafi Babar
project.description: A Claude Code plugin that takes a problem statement and drives a product through its full SDLC lifecycle — Strategy → Ideate → Design → Implement → Data → Quality → Deploy → Customer Validation — producing real, reviewable, methodology-compliant artifacts at every phase.

working_agreements.operator: Shafi Babar. Product Manager. Solo operator (Han Solo). Not a programmer. Provides problem statements, reviews artifacts, approves each chunk before proceeding.
working_agreements.claude_role: Principal engineer. Does all implementation — skills, agents, commands, hooks, tools, code generation. Shafi approves; Claude builds.
working_agreements.session_startup: Read sdlc-context.json first. Restore context from this file, not from conversation history.
working_agreements.chunk_gate: After Chunk 4: each subsequent chunk is planned, discussed, and explicitly approved by Shafi before execution. No chunk starts without a go.
working_agreements.build_discipline: One chunk at a time. One commit per chunk. Nothing in a later chunk is started while an earlier chunk is incomplete.
working_agreements.budget_posture: Frugal. Open-source preferred. No paid APIs, no expensive tooling. Process and methodology are the investment, not tooling.
working_agreements.timeline: Iterative. No deadlines. Quality over speed.

non_negotiables: Domain-Driven Design (DDD) — Ubiquitous Language, Bounded Contexts, Domain Events, Aggregates
Event Storming — applied at Design phase for every domain and subdomain
Test-Driven Development (TDD) — tests written before implementation code, always
Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) — Gherkin feature files for all acceptance criteria
SOLID — applied across all generated code artifacts
methodology.enforcement: Absence of an applicable methodology in any artifact is a defect, not a warning. Phase gate hooks enforce compliance before advancing.

tech_stack._note: These are the default target-stack decisions. Parameterized per product at /sdlc-start time. Saved to sdlc-config.json per product.

confirmed.api_framework: net/http + chi — idiomatic, lightweight, no magic, code-reviewable. Chosen over Gin/Echo/Fiber for compliance-grade transparency.
confirmed.primary_database: PostgreSQL + pgx — ACID, audit log, config store, outbox table. Default for all services.
confirmed.search_and_alerts: Elasticsearch — document indexing, full-text search, alert storage.
confirmed.message_broker: Redpanda — Kafka-API compatible, no JVM, frugal, familiar Kafka tooling.
confirmed.graph_database: Apache AGE (default, PostgreSQL extension, zero extra infra). Neo4j Community as selectable alternative when graph is primary product surface.
confirmed.ci_cd: GitHub Actions — native to GitHub repos, free tier, zero hosting overhead.
confirmed.iac: OpenTofu (infrastructure provisioning, open-source Terraform fork) + Helm (application deployment onto Kubernetes).
confirmed.service_mesh: Linkerd — Rust data plane, automatic mTLS, lowest resource overhead, CNCF graduated.
confirmed.container_orchestration: Kubernetes. EKS/AKS/GKE via OpenTofu for customer cloud. k3s for managed private tenants. kind for local development. Single Helm chart per service, environment differences in values.yaml.
confirmed.observability: OpenTelemetry (Go SDK) + Prometheus (metrics) + Elasticsearch + Fluent Bit (logs) + Tempo (traces) + Grafana (single pane). Kibana optional for deep log analysis.
confirmed.optional_database: MongoDB — variable-schema bounded contexts (entity extraction output, crawl metadata). Exposed as config option at /sdlc-start. Not default.

tech_stack.sdlc_start_questionnaire: Interactive questionnaire at /sdlc-start saves answers to sdlc-config.json per product. Allows per-product overrides of defaults above.

first_product.name: Data Estate Mapping and Compliance Intelligence
first_product.core_value: Maps an organisation's data estate across storage systems, extracts entities, builds a relationship graph, and provides compliance intelligence — with zero data ever leaving customer infrastructure.
first_product.architecture_style: Event-driven microservices with physical tenant isolation
first_product.pipeline: Worker nodes at customer storage → Message Queue → File Processing → Entity Extraction → Graph Update → Compliance Rule Engine → Alert + Audit
first_product.deployment_model: Option A: one-click deploy to customer's own AWS/Azure/GCP. Option B: managed private cloud with dedicated tenant, no data commingling.
first_product.security: Zero Trust, OAuth 2.0 + JWT, mTLS via Linkerd, ABAC claim-based authorization, API-First (UI is first-party API consumer only)
first_product.test_strategy: TDD as default. Test layers: Unit, Integration, Contract, E2E, Extraction quality, Security.
first_product.status: Not yet started — plugin must be built first.

plugin_architecture.description: Commands drive phases. Phases activate agents. Agents draw on skills for expertise and tools for atomic actions. Hooks enforce governance automatically. Artifacts are outputs of the workflow, not the inputs.
plugin_architecture.component_hierarchy: Command → Agent → Skill | Agent → Tool | Hook → Tool

axioms.Skills: Expertise — domain knowledge, standards, patterns. No reasoning.
axioms.Agents: Reasoning — decisions, analysis, design, code generation. Use skills for knowledge.
axioms.Commands: Workflows — user-facing phase drivers and cross-cutting operations.
axioms.Hooks: Governance — event-driven, lightweight, fast, idempotent. No business logic.

agents.name: ai-ml-architect
agents.status: deferred — added when a product requires ML

skill_domains._skills_note: 16 skills. go-concurrency-patterns, go-error-handling, go-performance-optimization added in Chunk 12 from Shafi's Go Backend & Observability Engineer behavioral blueprint (mechanical sympathy, leak-free concurrency, errors-as-values, profiling).

14 skills. react-performance-optimization and react-observability added in Chunk 13 from Shafi's React+TypeScript Frontend Engineer behavioral blueprint (critical rendering path, RUM/Core Web Vitals, OTel Web with W3C traceparent propagation, error boundaries).

skill_domains._scope_note: 12 skills (was 13 planned). Chunk 14 dropped go-security-test and go-compliance-test (owned by security-engineer via security-implementation + compliance-verification — avoiding overlap) and added test-pyramid (the shift-left/shift-right strategy spine). Organized around Shafi's shift-left (prevent bugs) / shift-right (validate resilience) objective; frugal tooling per Shafi's filtered SDET blueprint.

skill_domains._split_note: Domain co-owned. Instrumentation skills (opentelemetry-instrumentation, distributed-tracing-design, structured-logging-design, health-check-design) built in Chunk 12 by backend-engineer. Stack skills (prometheus-metrics-design, slo-definition, alerting-rules-design) pending in Chunk 15 with platform-engineer.

skill_domains.status: partial — glossary-management and methodology-review complete (Chunk 4); remaining four pending Chunk 18

commands._note: Phase driver commands activate the right agents in sequence, enforce entry conditions, and produce the full artifact set for that phase.

hooks._note: All hooks are fast (<2s), idempotent, and deterministic. Business logic belongs in agents and skills, not hooks.
planned: pre-phase-advance — validates all required artifacts exist before phase advances
post-artifact-created — updates sdlc-context.json when an artifact is created
validate-artifact-structure — validates frontmatter and required sections before save
terminology-drift-detector — checks canonical glossary terms are used
methodology-compliance-check — verifies DDD/TDD/BDD/SOLID presence where required
pre-code-generation — ensures architecture artifacts exist before code is written
pre-implement — ensures Design phase is complete before Implement phase starts
tdd-gate — ensures test files exist before implementation code (TDD enforcement)
naming-convention-enforcer — validates component names follow conventions
validate-sdlc-artifact — validates artifact structure and required fields
check-methodology-compliance — checks DDD/TDD/BDD/SOLID presence in an artifact
cross-reference-checker — verifies all referenced components exist

build_checklist.title: test-engineering/ skills + test-strategist agent
build_checklist.scope_note: Reshaped from 13 to 12 skills per Shafi's filtered SDET blueprint: dropped go-security-test + go-compliance-test (security-engineer owns these, Chunk 9), added test-pyramid (shift-left/shift-right strategy spine). Frugal tooling (schema-based contracts not Pact broker; toxiproxy not chaos platform; mutation periodic not per-PR). Built in two approved passes (shift-left, then shift-right). Boundary: test-strategist owns the discipline + cross-cutting/system tests; backend/frontend engineers apply unit/integration/component skills test-first.

build_checklist.title: ai-ml-architect agent + ML skills

decisions.decision: Plugin uses top-level directory structure (Option B — plugin spec layout)
decisions.rationale: Matches the Claude Code plugin specification exactly. Correct for distribution. MCP/LSP/monitors/themes follow the plugin manifest contract.
alternatives_rejected: Option A (.claude/ layout) — better for solo dev auto-discovery but wrong for a distributable plugin
Option C (both) — premature; add distribution packaging when needed

decisions.decision: Architecture pattern is workflow-first, not artifact-first
decisions.rationale: Commands drive phases. Agents activate within phases. Artifacts are outputs of the workflow. The previous build was artifact-first — a knowledge library, not a factory.
alternatives_rejected: Artifact-first — the old approach. Produced documents but no orchestration.

decisions.decision: Skills organized by knowledge domain, not SDLC phase
decisions.rationale: DDD, TDD, BDD apply across multiple phases. Phase-locked skills cause duplication or get skipped when the phase doesn't match. Domain organization makes skills reusable across phases.
alternatives_rejected: Phase-locked skills (strategy/, ideate/, design/, implement/ etc.) — used in old build, caused duplication

decisions.rationale: Every role named in SDLC-artifact-factory.md §1 is represented. data-engineer and security-engineer added from previous roster of 12 — they were missing from the initial plan. Each agent has single, non-overlapping responsibility.
alternatives_rejected: 12 agents — was missing data-engineer and security-engineer

decisions.decision: backend-engineer, frontend-engineer, and test-strategist produce full-range outputs including actual runnable code and executable tests
decisions.rationale: Shafi confirmed agents must produce real outputs — not just design documents. backend-engineer produces runnable Go code. frontend-engineer produces runnable React+TS code. test-strategist produces executable test files. All three also produce design and documentation outputs.
alternatives_rejected: Design-only outputs — insufficient; the plugin must produce shippable artifacts

decisions.rationale: The first product requires graph visualization (Obsidian-style) and compliance dashboards. React has mature graph libraries (D3, Cytoscape, Sigma.js). Component-level reactivity suits dashboards. HTMX+templ would create ongoing pain for the graph view.
alternatives_rejected: HTMX + templ — insufficient for complex graph visualization
Vue — viable but React has richer graph library ecosystem

decisions.decision: MCPs deferred — defined per product at /sdlc-start time
decisions.rationale: MCP servers are product-specific integration points. Building them as plugin-level defaults would be premature and potentially wrong for different products.
alternatives_rejected: Pre-built MCPs for GitHub, Jira, Google Drive — premature for a generic factory

decisions.decision: sdlc-context.json is the session memory — read at the start of every session
decisions.rationale: Context window compaction and new sessions lose conversation history. This file must be self-sufficient to restore full project context, architecture decisions, and build position.
alternatives_rejected: Relying on Claude's memory files only — insufficient for full architectural context

decisions.decision: Canonical component frontmatter schemas — SKILL.md: name/description/version/phase/owner/created/tags; AGENT.md: name/description/role/version/phase/owner/created/inputs/outputs/skills/tools/tags. Emitted artifact templates use the key 'name:', never 'artifact:'.
decisions.rationale: Three incompatible frontmatter shapes had emerged across build chunks (CLAUDE.md artifact standard, README component specs, on-disk reality). One machine-readable schema per component type restores introspectability and lets governance hooks validate uniformly.
alternatives_rejected: Keeping the lean Gen-2/3 agent frontmatter (name/description/version/phase/tags) — loses machine-readable inputs/outputs/skills/tools wiring
Renaming CLAUDE.md's required 'name' field to 'artifact' — would ripple into every governance rule

decisions.decision: Behavioral Directives in agents are an accepted pattern: a compressed index of imperative bullets, each citing the owning skill. Decision criteria/tables in skills are knowledge, not reasoning.
decisions.rationale: Codifies two boundary clarifications to the component anti-pattern rules. Directives whose substance is absent from the cited skill remain defects — the substance moves into the skill.
alternatives_rejected: Strict reading — stripping all directive content from agents into skills; loses the at-a-glance engineering contract without reducing duplication

decisions.decision: Content improvement campaign before Chunk 15: full per-file pass over all skills, agents, and repo metadata, delivered as five batch PRs.
decisions.rationale: A stronger model became available; three parallel audits found generational drift in agents, frontmatter schism, stale metadata, dead references, and terminology drift. Fixing before building Chunk 15 prevents propagating the drift into new components.
alternatives_rejected: Targeted fixes only — audit sampled ~20% of skills, so unsampled files would get no fresh scrutiny
Single campaign PR — a ~120-file diff is not PM-reviewable in one sitting

anti_patterns: Skill that reasons or decides — reasoning belongs in an Agent
Agent that stores large domain knowledge — knowledge belongs in Skills
Command that contains methodology or domain expertise — belongs in Skills
Hook that runs a workflow — hooks validate only, they do not orchestrate
Tool that makes business decisions — decisions belong in Agents
MCP that implements business workflows — MCPs expose atomic capabilities only
Agent with overlapping responsibilities — every agent has single, non-overlapping ownership
